summary: Reusable row for command-discovery and command-invocation results. The row keeps command identity, provenance, disabled reasons, and optional inline follow-up actions visible without requiring hover-only affordances.

hook_records.notes: Reference-theme row for default command-palette review.
Light theme is parity-quality, not a reduced shell mode.
High-contrast validation keeps focus, selection, and disabled reasons distinct.
The row consumes versioned appearance and theme-package truth rather than local CSS assumptions.
Live theme, contrast, and density changes resolve through the shared appearance session.

slots.description: Focus-owning row container inside the palette results list.
Canonical command or source icon shown before the label.
Primary command label and the start of the accessible name.
Context such as scope, category, or nearby path detail.
Freshness, approval, provenance, or partiality chips that materially change interpretation.
Shortcut or route hint aligned to the trailing edge.
Inline reason shown when the row is restricted, blocked, or otherwise not directly invokable.
Optional raw command id when support, docs, or extension provenance makes it worth showing exactly.
Optional trailing action such as open details or open docs.

responsive_reflow_notes: Secondary context and shortcut hint collapse before the primary label truncates.
Reason chips may wrap to a second line only when the palette surface is already in a two-line result mode.
Disabled reasons remain inline and never move to hover-only disclosure.

state_nodes.user_visible_summary: The row is ready for arrow navigation and invocation.
Pointer hover adds emphasis but does not reveal unique information.
Keyboard focus and current selection are both active on this row.
The row is inspectable but depends on stale or cached supporting truth.
Workspace trust narrowed the available action or supporting detail.
Administrative policy blocked direct invocation of the row's primary action.
The row remains visible and selectable, but the action is unavailable until the named condition changes.

transitions.user_visible_effect: Background emphasis increases without introducing hover-only meaning.
Focus ring and selection treatment move onto the row and the accessible description updates.
Freshness chip and explanation remain inline while the row stays keyboard reachable.
The row keeps its place in the list and adds a direct explanation of the narrowed action.
A policy reason and repair route replace direct invocation without hiding the row.
The row returns to normal invocation behavior without changing its list position.
Blocked messaging clears and the row is again directly invokable.

slot_content_rules.notes: The primary label remains the first readable identity and never disappears behind chips or shortcut hints.
Context may contain localized framing around a literal path, category, or support-class fragment.
Chips carry only information that materially changes how the user should interpret or trust the result.
Shortcut hints are secondary to identity and may disappear before the label truncates.
Blocked and degraded reasons remain inline and readable without hover.
The literal command id stays exact for support, docs, and extension-conformance flows.
Trailing actions stay textual enough to preserve accessibility and reduce icon-metaphor drift.

content_contract.blocked_and_degraded_copy_rule: When the row is stale, restricted, or policy blocked, the explanation stays inside the row and names what still works, what is narrowed, and where the user can recover.
content_contract.empty_or_missing_data_rule: If optional metadata such as a shortcut or secondary context is unavailable, the row preserves command identity and focus behavior instead of leaving a misleading blank badge area.

primary_navigation_keys.effect: Move focus to the next result row.
primary_navigation_keys.when: While focus remains in the command-palette results list.

primary_navigation_keys.effect: Move focus to the previous result row.
primary_navigation_keys.when: While focus remains in the command-palette results list.

primary_navigation_keys.effect: Move from the focused row to the optional inline action when one exists.
primary_navigation_keys.when: Only after the row itself already owns focus.

activation_keys.effect: Invoke the row's primary action or the host-defined safe preview path.
activation_keys.when: When the row surface owns focus and the row is not blocked.

activation_keys.effect: Open a row-local preview or details action when the host maps one for the result type.
activation_keys.when: Only for rows that advertise a preview path.

dismissal_keys.effect: Dismiss the palette and return focus to the invoking surface.
dismissal_keys.when: When no nested inline action menu is open.

interaction_contract.focus_return_rule: Dismissing the palette returns focus to the invoking surface or the nearest safe ancestor if the invoker disappeared while the palette was open.
interaction_contract.pointer_behavior_note: Hover may strengthen emphasis but must not be the only route to source badges, disabled reasons, or inline actions.

accessibility_contract.role_description: Option row inside the command-palette results list.
accessibility_contract.accessible_name_rule: The accessible name is composed from the primary label plus any visible source or disabled-reason text that materially changes interpretation.
accessibility_contract.accessible_description_rule: The accessible description appends secondary context, shortcut hint, and any approval, freshness, or policy chip in stable order.

announcement_rules.message_rule: The palette announces the updated result count once, then the focused row's disabled or stale reason only if that reason changed.
When the row becomes blocked or restricted, the repair or review route is appended to the description without moving focus.
The inline action exposes a distinct label and does not masquerade as row selection.

accessibility_contract.high_contrast_behavior: Focus ring, selection outline, and blocked-state explanations remain distinct in both high-contrast themes; chips flatten to text-plus-border treatments when needed.
accessibility_contract.forced_colors_behavior: Forced-colors mode preserves row focus, selection, and disabled meaning through borders, text, and icon treatment rather than hue.
accessibility_contract.blocked_and_degraded_behavior: Stale, restricted, and policy-blocked rows remain arrow-navigable and keep their explanation in the accessibility tree instead of relying on tooltip-only disclosure.

semantic_token_bindings.notes: Base surface, border, and text roles.
Focus and selection remain distinct from hover.
Blocked messaging keeps readable contrast and a load-bearing border.
Freshness and provenance chips stay legible without looking like primary actions.

density_variant_bindings.layout_effect: Row height tightens and trailing metadata collapses before the label does.
Reference density for screenshots and keyboard-review baselines.
Supporting text may breathe more, but the row keeps the same state vocabulary and focus order.

motion_bindings.reduced_motion_behavior: Hover and focus changes become instant; chip entrance and row shimmer are suppressed.

state_visualization_bindings.notes: Workspace-trust narrowing is distinct from admin policy.
Policy lock stays distinct from trust restriction and ordinary disabled state.

asset_slots.notes: Uses the command's canonical icon metaphor.
Freshness or blocked-state chips may include a semantic glyph.
The trailing action may stay text-only to preserve clarity.

localization_contract.text_expansion_strategy: Primary label truncates last. Secondary context and shortcut metadata collapse first, and two-line expansion is allowed only when the hosting palette already supports it.
localization_contract.bidi_behavior: Mixed-direction command ids or file paths remain isolated in code-style spans while surrounding explanatory copy follows the active locale direction.
localization_contract.formatting_behavior: Shortcut hints and any date text use locale-aware rendering when the host exposes it; command ids remain literal.
localization_contract.source_language_fallback_behavior: If an extension contributes only a source-language label, that fact remains visible instead of pretending translation parity.
localization_contract.notes: The component must remain usable in pseudoloc and mixed-direction command labels without clipping the disabled reason.

allowed_extension_variations: An extension-contributed row may add one provider or activation-cost chip when the host registry declares it.
An extension-contributed row may omit the trailing inline action when there is no safe secondary route.
extension_guidance.required_gap_disclosure: If an extension surface cannot show provenance, disabled reasons, or approval state inline, it must hand off to the host-owned command-palette row instead of rendering a misleading approximation.

substitution_and_deprecation.unsupported_package_behavior: If the active theme or extension package cannot map a required semantic role, the host row falls back to the first-party semantic role set and keeps the mismatch visible in the appearance/debug path.
